heading: Risk management
label: 60126 Risk management
notes: Deemed References to Chapters 509 and 511 of Title 51General references to “this title” deemed to refer also to chapters 509 and 511 of Title 51, National and Commercial Space Programs, see , set out as a note under .section 4(d)(8) of Pub. L. 111–314section 101 of this title

children.heading: Risk Management Program Demonstration Projects.—
children.label: a Risk Management Program Demonstration Projects.—
A
children.text: to demonstrate, through the voluntary participation by owners and operators of gas pipeline facilities and hazardous liquid pipeline facilities, the application of risk management; and

children.label: B
children.text: to evaluate the safety and cost-effectiveness of the program.

children.label: A
children.text: may exempt an owner or operator of the pipeline facility covered under the project (referred to in this subsection as a “covered pipeline facility”), from the applicability of all or a portion of the requirements under this chapter that would otherwise apply to the covered pipeline facility; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall exempt, for the period of the project, an owner or operator of the covered pipeline facility, from the applicability of any new standard that the Secretary promulgates under this chapter during the period of that participation, with respect to the covered facility.
invite owners and operators of pipeline facilities to submit risk management plans for timely approval by the Secretary;

require, as a condition of approval, that a risk management plan submitted under this subsection contain measures that are designed to achieve an equivalent or greater overall level of safety than would otherwise be achieved through compliance with the standards contained in this chapter or promulgated by the Secretary under this chapter;

children.label: A
children.text: collaborative government and industry training;

children.label: B
children.text: methods to measure the safety performance of risk management plans;

children.label: C
children.text: the development and application of new technologies;

children.label: D
children.text: the promotion of community awareness concerning how the overall level of safety will be maintained or enhanced by the demonstration project;

children.label: E
children.text: the development of models that categorize the risks inherent to each covered pipeline facility, taking into consideration the location, volume, pressure, and material transported or stored by that pipeline facility;

children.label: F
children.text: the application of risk assessment and risk management methodologies that are suitable to the inherent risks that are determined to exist through the use of models developed under subparagraph (E);

children.label: i
children.text: the owners and operators that participate in the demonstration project demonstrate that they are effectively managing the risks referred to in subparagraph (E); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the risk management plans carried out under the demonstration project under this subsection can be audited;

children.label: i
children.text: changed circumstances; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a determination by the Secretary that the owner or operator is not achieving an overall level of safety that is at least equivalent to the level that would otherwise be achieved through compliance with the standards contained in this chapter or promulgated by the Secretary under this chapter;

children.label: I
children.text: such other elements as the Secretary, with the agreement of the owners and operators that participate in the demonstration project under this section, determines to further the purposes of this section; and

children.label: J
children.text: an opportunity for public comment in the approval process; and

in selecting participants for the demonstration project, take into consideration the past safety and regulatory performance of each applicant who submits a risk management plan pursuant to paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Emergencies and Revocations
children.label: c .—Emergencies and Revocations
children.text: Nothing in this section diminishes or modifies the Secretary’s authority under this title to act in case of an emergency. The Secretary may revoke any exemption granted under this section for substantial noncompliance with the terms and conditions of an approved risk management plan.

children.heading: .—Participation by State Authority
children.label: d .—Participation by State Authority
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary may provide for consultation by a State that has in effect a certification under section 60105. To the extent that a demonstration project comprises an intrastate natural gas pipeline or an intrastate hazardous liquid pipeline facility, the Secretary may make an agreement with the State agency to carry out the duties of the Secretary for approval and administration of the project.
an evaluation of each such demonstration project, including an evaluation of the performance of each participant in that project with respect to safety and environmental protection; and

recommendations concerning whether the applications of risk management demonstrated under the demonstration project should be incorporated into the Federal pipeline safety program under this chapter on a permanent basis.
